steps.n: Added ي between the second and third letters to make it mean 'something that gives comfort' instead of just the action of feeling comfortable
Added the ٍ mark at the end to show this word describes what kind of garden it is - a garden of bliss
This is a basic particle that introduces a new topic or condition. It tells us we're about to focus on something specific.

steps.m: and as for (connects to previous statement)
steps.n: Added و at the beginning to connect this statement to what came before it

steps.m: and as for (connects to previous statement)
steps.n: Changed the final alif to a long alif (آ) which is just a writing variation - it doesn't change the meaning
This is a basic conditional particle that introduces a condition. In this ayah, it starts a phrase about someone's status or condition.
Added fathas to make it past tense. This shows the basic form before the weak letter change.
The و changes to ا because it's a hollow verb (has a weak middle letter)

steps.m: from/of (connects أَصْحٰبِ to what comes before it)
steps.n: This is a basic preposition that shows where something comes from or what something belongs to. In this ayah, it connects أَصْحٰبِ (companions/people) to what came before it.
Connected the root letters with vowels to make a basic past-tense verb
The word changed from 'to accompany' to 'companions' by making it plural using the pattern أَفْعَال
The kasra at the end shows this word belongs to or is possessed by something. Here, اصحاب (companions) belongs to اليمين (the right), forming the phrase 'companions of the right'.
Added vowels and the long ي sound to make the noun form meaning 'right side'

steps.m: the right side (possessed by أَصْحٰبِ - companions)
steps.n: Added 'the' at beginning and kasra at end because it's possessed by the word أَصْحٰبِ (companions) in the phrase 'companions of the right'

steps.m: to be safe and at peace

steps.n: Added an alif after the second letter to make it into a noun meaning 'peace'
Added فَ at the beginning to show this follows from what came before
Added double dammah to show this is indefinite peace
Starting with the basic preposition ل which means 'for' or 'to'
Adding shadda to ل adds emphasis, showing this is definitely or surely for someone
Adding ك at the end shows this is directed to one male person (you)
This is a basic preposition that shows where something comes from or belongs to. In this ayah, it connects 'you' to 'the companions of the right'
Adding vowels to make it a past tense verb meaning 'to accompany'
Changed from 'he accompanied' to 'one who accompanies' - like changing from a verb to a noun for the person doing it
Changed to plural form to show many companions
Added kasra at the end to show these companions belong to or are connected to something (in this case, اليَمِينِ - the right hand/side)
Added vowels and the ي after second letter to make it a noun meaning 'right side'
Added ٱلْ at beginning to make it definite (like 'the') and ِ at end to show it belongs to 'companions'
This is a conditional particle that introduces a new topic or contrast
Added و at the beginning to connect this phrase with what came before it
The final alif is lengthened to show a pause in reading
This is a conditional particle that shows the next part (كَانَ مِنَ ٱلْمُكَذِّبِينَ - if he was among the deniers) depends on something else
Added basic past tense vowels to show this happened in the past

steps.m: he was (of مِنَ ٱلْمُكَذِّبِينَ - from the deniers)
steps.n: The middle letter و changes to ا because it's between two vowels in a past tense verb. This is a common change in Arabic that makes the word easier to say.
This is a preposition that shows where something comes from or what group it belongs to
Added َ (fatha) at the end to connect smoothly to the next word الْمُكَذِّبِينَ (the deniers). This is like adding a connecting sound to make the words flow better.
Basic past tense verb pattern - added fat-ha (َ) marks to each letter to make 'he denied'
Adding shadda makes the meaning stronger
Adding مُ at the start and changing vowels turns a verb (action) into a noun meaning 'someone who does that action'
ين shows it's plural and comes after مِنَ (from) in the ayah
Added ٱلْ (the) at start + َ at end because it comes after مِنَ (from/of)

steps.m: to wander off the right path

steps.n: Changed the root into a noun by adding alif and shaddah to mean 'someone who strays'

steps.m: those who are lost/astray (owned, modifies المكذبين)
steps.n: The ين ending makes it plural (more than one person who is lost/astray) and shows it belongs to المكذبين
Adding ال makes it refer to specific lost ones. The ل gets a shadda because it comes after ال

steps.m: the ones who are lost/astray (acted on)
steps.n: The َ (fatḥa) at the end shows this word is being acted upon in the sentence
Changes from verb 'to come down' to noun meaning 'what is provided to guests'

steps.m: then hospitality (what is provided for guests)
steps.n: Adds فَ (meaning 'then/so') at start and tanween damma at end to show it's indefinite
This is a preposition that shows the starting point or source of something
The م gets doubled (shadda) because it comes after a word ending in ن, making the م sound stronger - this is a common rule in Arabic pronunciation
Connected the root letters with fatha to create the basic past tense verb
The pattern with ي in the middle shows something that has this quality very strongly

steps.m: boiling water (possessed by something mentioned before)
steps.n: The word gets two dots under it (ٍ) to show it belongs to something mentioned before, like saying 'water of' something
Changes from basic root into a noun meaning 'the act of doing something'

steps.m: and the act of burning intensively
steps.n: Added و at beginning to connect this word to what came before it

steps.m: and the act of burning intensively (subject of sentence)
steps.n: Added ُ to show this is the subject doing the action in the sentence
Connected the root letters with fatha to form the basic past tense verb
Added ي between second and third root letters and changed middle vowel to kasra to create an intensive noun showing something with a strong quality
Added the -in sound at the end to show it belongs to or connects with another word, making it mean 'of hellfire'
This is a common particle used at the start of sentences to add emphasis

Adding the shadda (ّ) makes the word stronger and changes it from possibly meaning 'if' to definitely meaning 'indeed/certainly'. Here it emphasizes the following statement 'this is the truth of certainty'.
Basic demonstrative element meaning 'here' or 'this'
Added ذا to specify this is for pointing to something masculine and nearby

steps.m: this (pointing to something nearby in إِنَّ هٰذَا لَهُوَ حَقُّ ٱلْيَقِينِ)
steps.n: Added alif with overbar (ٰ) and fatha marks to complete the word. In this ayah, it's pointing to what was just mentioned before.
Basic pronoun for 'he' or 'it'
The لَ adds emphasis to connect with حَقُّ (truth) that follows it, showing this is definitely/surely the truth

steps.m: to be firm, established, or true
steps.n: Root meaning relates to truth and reality
The middle letter gets a shadda to create a noun meaning 'truth' or 'reality'
Added damma at the end because this word is the predicate (tells us what something is) after the word لَهُوَ (it is)
Added ي after second root letter to turn the verb into a noun meaning the quality itself
Added ال to make it definite and ِ to show it belongs with the word حَقُّ (truth) that comes before it

steps.m: to glorify, to praise by declaring perfection

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make basic past tense verb
Doubling the middle letter makes the action stronger and more emphatic
Adding sukun at the end makes it a command to someone

steps.m: so glorify! (in response to what was mentioned before)
steps.n: Added فَ at beginning to show this is a response or result

steps.m: to name something, give a name

steps.n: Changes from verb 'to name' into the noun 'name' by adding vowel marks
The weak letter و changes to nothing after adding the alif prefix
Added بِ at the beginning to mean 'by/with', ٱ (which is like ال) to mean 'the', and ِ at the end to show it connects to the word after it (رَبِّكَ meaning 'your Lord')
The doubled middle letter (shadda) makes this a noun for someone who does the action intensively, emphasizing the complete nature of the nurturing
The كَ at the end means 'your' (speaking to one male). The word is connected to بِٱسْمِ (in the name of) which is why it has the ِ mark under ب

steps.m: to be magnificent/great in size or status

steps.n: Added vowels (fatha-damma-fatha) to the root letters to make it mean 'became great' in the past
Changed to an adjective describing something with this quality
Added the definite article to specify this particular greatness
Added kasra at the end to show this describes 'your Lord' in the phrase 'name of your Lord, the Magnificent'
